tool_use.name: Edit
tool_use.param:
## Adding New Bindings
1. Check this table for conflicts 2. Prefer hidden bindings for power-user shortcuts 3. Follow the `action_<verb>_<noun>` naming pattern 4. Update this document after adding

## Priority Binding Pattern for Widget Override
<!-- Source: src/erk/tui/screens/objectiv...[truncated 781 chars]...s 3. Follow the `action_<verb>_<noun>` naming pattern 4. Update this document after adding

session.tool_result: <tool_use_error>File has not been read yet. Read it first before writing to it.</tool_use_error>

1→--- 2→title: TUI Keyboard Shortcuts Inventory 3→read_when: 4→ - "adding a new keyboard shortcut to the TUI" 5→ - "checking for shortcut conflicts before binding a new key" 6→ - "understanding what keys are available in the TUI" 7→tripwires: 8→ - action: "adding a new key binding without checking existing bindings" 9→ warning: "Check this document and ErkDashApp.BINDINGS in app.py for conflicts. Some keys are hidden but still active." 10→ score: 4 11→--- 12→ 13→# TUI Keyboard Shortcuts Inventory 14→ 15→Complete binding inventory from `ErkDashApp.BINDINGS` in `src/erk/tui/app.py`. 16→ 17→## All Bindings 18→ 19→<!-- Source: src/erk/tui/app.py, ErkDashApp.BINDINGS --> 20→ 21→The complete binding inventory is defined in `ErkDashApp.BINDINGS`. The bindings include both visible shortcuts (shown in help) and hidden power-user shortcuts. 22→ 23→## Key Groups 24→ 25→**Navigation:** j/k (vim-style), escape (quit) 26→ 27→**View Switching:** 1/2/3 (direct), left/right (cycle) 28→ 29→**Filters:** o (objective), t (stack), / (text filter), s (sort) 30→
... [40 lines omitted] ...

session.tool_result:
1→--- 2→title: Textual CommandPalette Guide 3→read_when: 4→ - "implementing command palette in Textual TUI" 5→ - "hiding system commands from command palette" 6→ - "get_system_commands method" 7→ - "removing Keys Quit Screenshot Theme from palette" 8→ - "adding emoji prefixes to command palette entries" 9→ - "using CommandCategory for command categorization" 10→last_audited: "2026-02-05 00:00 PT" 11→audit_result: edited 12→--- 13→ 14→# Textual CommandPalette Guide 15→ 16→For standard Textual CommandPalette patterns (Providers, Hit/DiscoveryHit, Matcher, async generators, Screen.COMMANDS), consult the [Textual documentation](https://textual.textualize.io/). This doc focuses on erk-specific patterns and a critical Textual pitfall. 17→ 18→## System Commands Pitfall 19→ 20→**CRITICAL:** `get_system_commands` must be overridden on the **App class**, not the Screen class. Textual calls `app.get_system_commands(screen)` when opening the palette — it never calls this method on screens. 21→ 22→```python 23→# WRONG — This method is never called by Textual 24→class MyModalScreen(ModalScreen): 25→ def get_system_commands(self, screen: Screen) -> Iterator[SystemCommand]: 26→ return iter(()) # Has no effect! 27→ 28→# CORRECT — Override on App class 29→class MyApp(App): 30→ def get_system_commands(self, screen: Screen) -> Iterator[SystemCommand]:
... [39 lines omitted] ...

1→# CLI Push Down Pattern 2→ 3→This pattern is documented in the `cli-push-down` skill. 4→ 5→Load it with: `$cli-push-down` 6→ 7→See `.claude/skills/cli-push-down/SKILL.md` for the full pattern documentation including decision rules, implementation checklist, case studies, and anti-patterns. 8→
<system-reminder> Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior. </system-reminder>

session.assistant: I need to add tripwires to the git-plumbing-patterns.md and git-graphite-quirks.md frontmatter. Let me read the current frontmatter of those files.
session.tool_result:
1→--- 2→title: Git Plumbing Patterns 3→read_when: 4→ - "modifying plan_save.py branch commit behavior" 5→ - "understanding git plumbing patterns in erk" 6→ - "working with commit_files_to_branch" 7→ - "eliminating git checkouts" 8→ - "dispatch without checkout" 9→tripwires: 10→ - action: "checking out a branch in plan_save to commit files" 11→ warning: "Plan save uses git plumbing (commit_files_to_branch) to commit without checkout. Do NOT add checkout_branch calls. See git-plumbing-patterns.md." 12→ - action: "adding new git operations that require a branch checkout" 13→ warning: "When adding new git operations, prefer plumbing (update-ref, commit-tree) over checkout-based workflows. See git-plumbing-patterns.md." 14→--- 15→
<system-reminder> Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior. </system-reminder>

1→--- 2→title: Git and Graphite Edge Cases Catalog 3→last_audited: "2026-02-13 00:00 PT" 4→audit_result: edited 5→read_when: 6→ - "debugging unexpected git/gt behavior" 7→ - "handling rebase/restack edge cases" 8→ - "writing conflict detection logic" 9→ - "troubleshooting detached HEAD states" 10→ - "handling concurrent worktree operations" 11→ - "understanding worktree lock files" 12→tripwires: 13→ - action: "calling gt commands without --no-interactive flag" 14→ warning: "Always use `--no-interactive` with gt commands (gt sync, gt submit, gt restack, etc.). Without this flag, gt may prompt for user input and hang indefinitely. Note: `--force` does NOT prevent prompts - you must use `--no-interactive` separately." 15→ pattern: "\\bgt\\s+(sync|submit|restack|create|modify)" 16→ - action: "calling graphite.track_branch() with a remote ref like origin/main" 17→ warning: "Graphite's `gt track` only accepts local branch names, not remote refs. Use BranchManager.create_branch() which normalizes refs automatically, or strip `origin/` prefix before calling track_branch()." 18→ - action: "using `gt restack` to resolve branch divergence errors" 19→ warning: "gt restack only handles parent-child stack rebasing, NOT same-branch remote divergence. Use git rebase origin/$BRANCH first." 20→ pattern: "gt\\s+restack" 21→ - action: "using git pull or git pull --rebase on a Graphite-managed branch" 22→ warning: "Use /erk:diverge-fix instead. git pull --rebase rewrites commit SHAs outside Graphite's tracking, causing stack divergence that requires manual cleanup with gt sync --restack and force-push." 23→ pattern: "git\\s+pull" 24→ - action: "comparing git SHA to Graphite's tracked SHA for divergence detection" 25→ warning: "Ensure both `commit_sha` and `graphite_tracked_sha` are non-None before comparison. Returning False when either is None avoids false negatives on new branches." 26→ - action: "amending a commit when Graphite is enabled" 27→ warning: "After amending commits or running gt restack, Graphite's cache may not update, leaving branches diverged. Call retrack_branch() to fix tracking. The auto-fix is already implemented in checkout_cmd, rewrite_cmd, submit_pipeline, and branch_manager." 28→ - action: "using --force-with-lease in multi-step workflows where earlier steps push" 29→ warning: "Force-push silently overwrites intermediate commits from earlier workflow steps. Always `git pull --rebase` before pushing in multi-step workflows." 30→ - action: "using git merge on a Graphite-managed branch"
... [10 lines omitted] ...
31→ warning: "Merge commits break Graphite's linear stack model. Use git pull --rebase or gt sync. Merge commits cause gt squash divergence errors and broken parent tracking." 33→ warning: "Git refuses to force-update the checked-out branch. Use LBYL check: compare target branch with current branch before force-update. See _ensure_local_matches_remote() in graphite.py."
